Alert #243 — Reconnaissance DETECTED (80% confidence) ⚠ MODEL DISAGREEMENT — RF and IF gave opposing verdicts. Manual verification required.

MITRE Technique: T1595 – Active Scanning

ML Scores:
  Isolation Forest : -0.052  (within baseline)
  Random Forest    : 99.7% attack probability

Investigation Priority Actions:
  1. Identify if scan is targeted (specific hosts) or broad (subnet sweep)
  2. Check if source IP is internal — may indicate compromised host
  3. Correlate with T1595 — active scanning behaviour
  4. Review DNS query logs for domain enumeration patterns

Escalation Trigger:
  Escalate to Security Engineer if any of the following:
  • Lateral movement indicators found
  • More than one host affected
  • Data exfiltration signs detected
  • Alert matches known threat actor TTPs
recEngineer:
Remediation Guidance — Sample #243 [Reconnaissance]

Technique: T1595 – Active Scanning
Confidence: 80%  |  IF Score: -0.052  |  Models Agree: No — review before acting

Remediation Steps:
  1. Block source IP and add to threat intelligence feed
  2. Enable network deception — redirect scans to honeypot
  3. Review externally exposed services — disable unused ones
  4. Update SIEM correlation rule for scan-then-exploit sequences

Detection Tuning:
  If this is confirmed true positive:
  • Add source IP to threat intelligence blocklist
  • Create SIEM correlation rule for this specific pattern
  • Update IDS signatures if signature-based detection missed it

  If this is confirmed false positive:
  • Whitelist source — document in false positive register
  • Tune Isolation Forest threshold (currently calibrated at 95th percentile)
  • Retrain RF with updated labelled data if pattern is systematic
